Why local-first matters

By Jane Doe — March 2024

The cloud is convenient. But when a service shuts down, your data goes with it. Local-first software flips the default: your data lives on your machine, and the network is an optional accelerant.

What changes

You stop renting access to your own information. Backups become trivial. Offline becomes free. And critically, you can choose when to sync.

This is not nostalgia. It is just better defaults for a class of small, personal tools.

  • You own the database file.
  • Search runs on your machine.
  • Backups are a copy command.
